entry.title: How to: Adding Product Hierarchy to analytical CDS Views
entry.content:
In the following post, I will show you how to extend the CDS version of the Fiori App: Market Segments - Actuals and Market Segments - Plan/Actual. This can be, however, used for any CDS View if you know the name of the CDS view. 

Please change the naming of the sqlViewAppendName and the WITH statement based on your naming convention, as every name must be unique.

First of all, you need to extend this CDS View E_JournalEntryItem and E_FinancialPlanningEntryItem:

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXEJOURNALITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for View E_JournalEntryItem'
extend view E_JournalEntryItem with ZXX_JOURNALENTRYITEM_PRODHPA
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_SalesOffice as _SalesOffice on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOffice.SalesOffice
association [0..1] to I_SalesOfficeText as _SalesOfficeText on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOfficeText.SalesOfficeName
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  Persistence.prodh_pa as prodh_pa,
  
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_SalesOffice'
  Persistence.vkbur_pa as vkbur_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText,
  _SalesOffice,
  _SalesOfficeText
}

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXEJOURNALITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for View E_FinancialPlanEntIt'
extend view E_FinancialPlanningEntryItem with ZXX_E_Financialplanningentr
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  Persistence.prodh_pa as prodh_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText
}

 

 

This way, you will enable it for the I_JournalEntryItemCube and I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube. However, these two need to be extended as well. The first extension was just to propagate the field.

Now you need to do these two extensions for I_JournalEntryItemCube and I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube:

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXIJRNLENTITCU'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension of I_JournalEntryItemCube of Prodh_PA/VKBUR_PA'
extend view I_JournalEntryItemCube with  ZXX_I_JournaEntryItCuProdHier
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_SalesOffice as _SalesOffice on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOffice.SalesOffice
association [0..1] to I_SalesOfficeText as _SalesOfficeText on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOfficeText.SalesOfficeName
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  _Extension.prodh_pa,
  
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_SalesOffice'
  _Extension.vkbur_pa,

  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText,
  _SalesOffice,
  _SalesOfficeText
}
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXJRNITMCUBE'
@EndUserText.label: 'Journal Entry cube extension'
extend view I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube with ZXX_PRODH_PA
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..*] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
{
   .textArrangement: #TEXT_FIRST
    @AbapCatalog.compiler.caseJoin
    @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
   case 
    when APJEI.ActualPlanCode ='A'
        then _Extension_acdoca.prodh_pa
    when APJEI.ActualPlanCode ='P'
        then _Extension_acdocp.prodh_pa
   end as prodh_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText
}

 

With this you are now able to use both fields in the cubes. As the last step you will extend the consumption CDS view. 

This extension is for C_MarketSegmentsQ2501 which is Market Segments - Actuals.

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXMARKETPLAN'
@EndUserText.label: 'market segment ext'
extend view C_MarketSegmentQ2501  with ZFO_E_MarketSegmentQ2501
{
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  prodh_pa,
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  vkbur_pa
    
}

 

 

 

 

 This extension is for C_MarketSegmentsPlanActQ2501 which is Market Segments - Plan/Actuals.

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXMARKETACTPLAN'
@EndUserText.label: 'Market Segments - Plan/Actuals - Ext PRODH_PA'
extend view C_MarketSegmentPlanActQ2501 with ZXX_E_MarketSegmentsPlanAct

{
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
@AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
@AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT

prodh_pa

}

 

When you have all these extensions in your system you will see the Product Hierarchy with description in the query, if selected.

Result of extensions in Market Segments - Actuals

entry.title: Thoughts About Generative AI in Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Reciprocal Relationship Between Master Data Governance and Generative Artificial Intelligence

In this blog post, I'd like to point out how generative AI impacts master data management and vice versa.

Master Data Governance Facilitating Generative Artificial Intelligence

In simple terms, generative AI is meant to automatically create reliable output in response to user prompts. With regards to master data governance there are multiple dimensions to this. On the one hand, master data governance is the foundation that ensures clean, consistent, and reliable master data, which is crucial for fact-based decision-making and flawless business transactions across organizations. Such a foundation is also pivotal in an AI context. Any generative AI output depends on the quality of the underlying source data from large language models (LLM). In a business context, training data is often based on corporate data, including master data. Poor data quality can lead to so-called AI hallucinations (like what is known as "garbage in/garbage out" from an analytical perspective). This means in a nutshell that the accuracy of LLM-derived responses depends heavily on the quality of the prompts and the underlying data quality itself. This simply means that the importance of effective master data governance becomes even more relevant when generative AI is applied in a business context, in which master data provides a critical foundation. To find out more about the role of master data governance in an AI context, read this article or listen to this podcast.

Generative Artificial Intelligence Facilitating Master Data Governance 

On the other hand, state-of-the art master data governance must be able to cope with challenges such as processing large data volumes with as little manual intervention as possible. And the good thing is that SAP Master Data Governance features various means to this end already, for example:

  • ML-based rule mining for the automated detection of hidden patterns in master data and the resulting proposal and definition of additional data quality rules
  • Derivation rules for automated field population
  • Data provider integration for the automated take-over of proven source data

Now, with SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, the use of generative AI is an additional capability to support automation and consumer-grade user experience.

Note: You may also watch the SAP let’s talk data podcast about the value of automation in MDM (with Andreas Seifried)

Generative AI in central governance (classic mode)

Generative AI is applied in central governance (classic mode) for summary of changes and assisted changes. To get an idea of how this works, watch the short video:

Video showing gen AI applied in central governance (classic mode)

 

To watch the video, click on the picture above. 

Generative AI in business partner processing (cloud-ready mode)

In addition, generative AI can be used in business partner processing (cloud-ready mode) initiated by user prompts using the SAP Joule copilot.

User prompts in Joule copilot and follow-ups in MDG

 This integration enables:

  • User prompts and dialog in natural language with AI copilot Joule to get details on business partner data.
  • Option to directly call MDG for follow-up processing in cloud-ready mode.

The AI features described in this blog post are only available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (not in the on-premise version).

As pointed out before, the impact of a consistent master data layer for generative AI based output is huge while the journey of AI integration in SAP Master Data Governance processes has only just begun. There is more to come. Stay tuned. 

Best,
Markus

entry.title: What is SAP Asset & Service Management?
entry.content:
What is SAP Asset & Service Management?

SAP Asset & Service Management is a comprehensive, AI-powered suite for managing the entire asset lifecycle while driving operational excellence. This blog explores how it is setting new benchmarks in asset performance and reliability—and yes, there’s a demo video to showcase its impact.

Across asset-intensive industries, SAP Asset & Service Management delivers intelligent solutions to solve real-world challenges and unlock value. From oil, gas, & energy to utilities, travel/transportation, mining, manufacturing, aerospace & defense, and beyond, SAP is the trusted partner for organizations aiming to stay ahead.

Our solution combines advanced AI and automation with practical tools to optimize every aspect of asset operations—from strategic planning and predictive maintenance to efficient scheduling and seamless execution. With SAP, businesses don’t just manage assets; they drive operational excellence, improve sustainability, and continuously improve with closed feedback loops.

Watch this quick demo video to see SAP in action—empowering organizations to help their assets run better:

Reflecting high customer satisfaction in the enterprise asset management (EAM) market, IDC has awarded SAP the 2024 SaaS EAM Customer Satisfaction Award. This recognition underscores SAP’s dedication to delivering innovative solutions that empower organizations to achieve operational excellence and long-term sustainability.

Additionally, SAP has been recognized as a Leader in both the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Field Service Management Applications 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989523, December 2023) and IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Service Life-Cycle Management Platforms 2023-2024 Vendor Assessment (doc #US49989623, October 2023). Learn more about what analysts are saying about Asset & Service Management from SAP.

Key Strengths of SAP Asset & Service Management

Integral Part of SAP's ERP Suite

SAP Asset & Service Management is deeply embedded in the core ERP suite, offering advanced capabilities for Asset Performance Management and Field Service Management. This embedded approach ensures that complex asset and service requirements are met within a unified business environment.

SAP Asset & Service Management

AI-Infused Asset Lifecycle Management

SAP Business AI transforms the asset lifecycle, enabling predictive failure analysis, optimized scheduling, and actionable insights through tools like SAP’s Co-Pilot, Joule. These innovations simplify processes, enhance decision-making, and automate tasks, helping organizations proactively address challenges and adapt to changing demands.

AI-infused Asset & Service Management


A Unified Asset Data Thread

A digital thread of asset data ensures seamless integration of technical and operational information, creating a single source of truth for asset management. By unifying data across systems, this approach enhances governance, ensures accuracy, and enables organizations to make informed decisions while aligning with industry standards.

Integrate Asset & Service Management accross the enterprise

Closed-Loop Asset Management

A closed-loop asset management approach integrates asset data, risk assessments, maintenance strategies, and execution insights into a continuous improvement cycle. By leveraging IoT insights, advanced analytics, and automated workflows, it optimizes reliability, minimizes downtime, and enhances safety while aligning with ESG goals. The result is smarter, more sustainable asset performance with ongoing improvements.

Closed Loop Asset Lifecycle Management

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Organizations can enhance reliability and manage risks at scale with a solution that identifies critical assets, prioritizes risk, and supports systematic maintenance strategies. This approach enables improved ESG outcomes and minimizes disruptions through smarter asset management.

Asset Reliability Engineering & Risk Management

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

Dynamic planning tools from SAP balance maintenance demands with available resources, making them ideal for Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) activities. Scenario-based simulations enable organizations to optimize schedules, reduce costs, and ensure timely maintenance while minimizing downtime.

Maintenance Planning and Optimization

EAM and FSM Convergence

SAP bridges the gap between Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) and Field Service Management (FSM), delivering a holistic solution for managing both internal and customer assets. This unified approach enhances operational efficiency and optimizes equipment performance across the board.

EAM and FSM Convergence

Sustainable and Safe Operations

Embedded sustainability and safety features ensure maintenance practices are compliant, safe, and aligned with regulatory goals. These capabilities span risk management, regulatory and permit compliance, and optimizing resource assignments through predictive routing to reduce travel times and CO₂ emissions. They also help prevent environmentally damaging asset failures while extending the useful life of assets and supporting long-term operational sustainability.

Sustainability and Compliance as Core Principles

Mobile Work Management

According to the US Bureau of Labor Statistics, job openings in installation, maintenance, and repair occupations are projected to grow significantly in the coming years. Coupled with a shortage of skilled labour, this makes mobile work management tools more critical than ever. SAP's mobile work management solutions enable field technicians to access work orders, asset and customer data, and instructions in real time—whether online or offline. With connected data and intuitive workflows, mobile workers are empowered to perform at their best.

Mobile Work Management

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

With agile asset health monitoring and advanced analytics, SAP Asset Performance Management enables organizations to proactively manage asset health, uncover trends, and address issues before they escalate. By enhancing reliability, reducing unplanned downtime, and extending asset lifecycles, it delivers smarter, data-driven insights that optimize asset performance. 

Comprehensive Health Monitoring and Analytics

Collaborative Ecosystem and Business Networks

Collaboration is at the heart of effective asset management. An adaptable, extensible platform allows stakeholders to seamlessly share asset data and drive collaborative work management processes tailored to industry needs. Furthermore, partner solutions like Cumulocity IoT, Locana, and AsInt further enhance capabilities, enabling organizations to achieve mutual goals in asset performance and operational excellence.

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

The true measure of SAP Asset & Service Management’s impact is reflected in our customers' success. Discover how companies are revolutionizing their asset/service management practices and achieving operational excellence with SAP:

  • Equinor Boosts Asset Performance with Condition-Based Maintenance Enabled by SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Freeport-McMoRan: Using Mobile Apps to Make Asset Maintenance Faster, Safer, and More Efficient
  • Patterson Dental: How Does Integrated Field Service Management Help Save Time and Cut Costs?
  • Swiss Federal Railways Speeds Ahead with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

Organizations worldwide are unlocking the potential of SAP’s asset management solutions, improving reliability, boosting operational efficiency, and achieving sustainability goals—all with smarter, AI-powered tools designed for the future.

Ready to transform your asset and service management? Visit www.sap.com/eam to explore solutions, or register for our upcoming webinars for the latest insights.

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. For more information about SAP Asset & Service Management solutions, visit www.sap.com/eam. I also encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Intelligence: SAP Business AI, Joule, and Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a crucial driving force for innovation and growth. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, offers a suite of AI solutions tailored to meet the diverse needs of modern businesses. Among these, SAP Business AI stands out, with two primary offerings:  Joule and Embedded AI. This blog explores the distinctions between these two powerful solutions, highlighting their unique features and capabilities.

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

What is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI represents a collection of intelligent technologies designed to automate and optimize business processes within the enterprise environment. By leveraging machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced AI techniques, SAP Business AI enables organizations to make data-driven decisions faster.

Picture1: Key features of SAP Business AI

Key Features of SAP Business AI

  1. Embedded AI: SAP embeds AI capabilities directly into its existing solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP C/4HANA, and SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring seamless user experience and quick adoption.
  2. Automated Machine Learning: SAP Business AI leverages automated machine learning to build and train models rapidly, without the need for extensive data science expertise.
  3. Natural Language Processing (NLP): It uses NLP to enable conversational AI, allowing users to interact with SAP systems using natural language.
  4. Robotic Process Automation (RPA): SAP Business AI employs RPA to automate repetitive tasks, freeing up human resources for more strategic work.
  5. Predictive Analytics: It offers predictive analytics to help businesses anticipate future trends, customer behavior, and market changes.

This blog is going to focus on Joule and Embedded AI and how they are used in SAP S/4HANA to improve user experience and make product extensions easier and more efficient.

Understanding Joule

Joule is a platform that allows businesses to develop, deploy, and manage AI applications with ease. It serves as a comprehensive toolbox for AI and Machine Learning (ML), providing developers with the necessary resources to build intelligent solutions tailored to specific business needs. Key features of Joule include:

  1. Flexible Development: Joule offers a versatile environment for developing AI models using preferred tools and frameworks. This flexibility empowers developers to create custom AI solutions that seamlessly integrate with existing systems.
  2. Scalability: The platform supports scalable deployment of AI applications, ensuring that businesses can handle increasing volumes of data and user requests efficiently.
  3. Ecosystem Compatibility: Joule is designed to work well with various SAP and third-party applications, fostering a collaborative ecosystem where different tools and technologies can interoperate.

In the ever-evolving landscape of enterprise resource planning, SAP S/4HANA has emerged as a powerful solution, offering a wide range of functionalities to streamline business processes. However, navigating through its complex interface can sometimes be challenging for users. This is where Joule comes into play. Joule is an innovative tool designed to simplify navigation in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing user experience and productivity.

Joule is a user-friendly navigation tool that helps users quickly find and access the required transactions, reports, and other functionalities within SAP S/4HANA. It acts as a search engine within the SAP environment, making it easier for users to locate specific features without having to memorize transaction codes or navigate through multiple menus.

 Key Features of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (I don’t know if you want to include Private as well – if yes, keep it as SAP S/4HANA Cloud)

  1. Intuitive Search: Joule allows users to perform keyword-based searches. Whether you're looking for a specific transaction code, report, or even a custom SAP program, Joule can find it for you in a matter of seconds.
  2. Smart Suggestions: As you type in the search bar, Joule provides smart suggestions based on your input. This feature significantly reduces the time spent searching for the right option, enhancing overall efficiency.
  3. Personalized Experience: Joule learns from your usage patterns and adapts to your preferences. It keeps track of frequently used transactions and reports, making them easily accessible from the home screen.
  4. Integrated Navigation: Joule integrates seamlessly with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud interface, providing a unified navigation experience. This integration ensures that users can access all necessary functionalities without having to switch between different tools or interfaces.
  5. Accessibility: Joule is accessible from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud home screen, making it convenient for users to start their search right from the main interface. This eliminates the need to open separate applications or modules.

Benefits of Using Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Improved Productivity: With Joule, users can quickly find what they need, reducing the time spent on navigation. This leads to increased productivity and efficiency.
  2. Reduced Training Time: New users can easily adapt to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with Joule's intuitive interface. The tool's simplicity and user-friendliness reduce the learning curve, making training more efficient.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Joule provides a seamless and personalized experience, tailored to each user's needs. This enhances overall satisfaction and makes the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system more approachable.
  4. Better Decision Making: By making it easier to access reports and analytics, Joule helps users make informed decisions promptly. This is particularly beneficial for managers and executives who need quick access to specific data.

Steps to Use Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access Joule: From the SAP S/4 HANA home screen, locate the Joule search bar. This is usually placed in a prominent position for easy access.
  2. Enter Keywords: Type in the keywords related to the transaction, report, or functionality you are looking for. As you type, Joule will provide relevant suggestions.
  3. Select the Desired Option: From the list of suggestions, select the desired option. Joule will navigate you directly to the relevant screen or transaction.

The screen shot below shows an example of how Joule can be used to quickly find information in your S/4HANA Cloud system. In this case the user wants to know how to create a Sales Order in the system.  Instead of having to navigate through the help menus the user can simply type “How do I create a sales order” in the Joule editor and Joule will display all the information necessary for the user to get started.

 

Figure 2: Sample of navigation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Joule

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

Figure 3: Form before the changes

 

Figure 4: Form after the changes

Conclusion

SAP Business AI offers a powerful suite of tools to help businesses achieve their digital transformation goals. Joule provides a flexible platform for easy navigation and fast data retrieval.  Joule can also be used to develop and deploy custom AI solutions, catering to the needs of developers and data scientists. In contrast, Embedded AI focuses on delivering ready-to-use AI functionalities integrated within existing SAP applications, providing immediate value to end-users.

Joule and Embedded AI can be used concurrently to simplify the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system by allowing users to retrieve data and perform operations faster and more efficiently.

For organizations looking to build custom AI applications, Joule also offers unparalleled flexibility and scalability. On the other hand, Embedded AI is ideal for businesses seeking quick, out-of-the-box AI solutions to enhance their existing SAP environments.

Ultimately, both Joule and Embedded AI play crucial roles in the SAP Business AI ecosystem, empowering businesses to unlock the full potential of artificial intelligence and drive sustainable growth.

Stay tuned for more insights into the evolving world of enterprise AI and its transformative impact on modern business operations.

entry.title: Billing Relevance G in execution order items in Service with Advanced Execution
entry.content:
The concept of Billing Relevance in Service with Advanced Execution is already explained in a previous blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/billing-relevance-in-execution-order-item/ba-p/13574529

This blog is to showcase the Service with Advanced execution process flow with billing relevance which is Itemized Billing (Key = G). This billing method is only available with advanced execution.

Itemized Billing is a billing scenario which uses the dynamic item processor (DIP) to bill services. This is a variant of resource-related billing which means customers are charged based on actuals.

In general, the price for tailor-made services can neither be defined in a service contract nor in the pricing master.

Typical examples of this are:

  • Industries which generally charge on actuals,
  • Specific services, such as consulting

In these cases, customers are billed for the resources consumed (for example, personnel, material, travel) to carry out the services. In resource-related billing, the system creates dynamic items from the cost information on the maintenance order.

The following steps depicts the process flow for Itemized Billing:

1. Create a service order and add an execution order item.

Service Order with an execution order item

2. Navigate to the item overview page in Edit mode, scroll down to the Billing assignment block and change Billing Relevance to Itemized Billing and enter a DIP Profile.

The dynamic item processor (DIP) profile is mandatory in the case of resource-related billing. During billing, the dynamic item processor determines the items or lines in the debit memo request, from the costs on the maintenance order, based on the DIP Profile entered in the execution order item in the service order.

Note: The DIP Profile entered in the service order must be assigned with Usage 1 Billing.

Billing assignment block in an execution order item

3. Release the item and save the service order.

Item status after creation of maintenance order

Maintenance order is generated, and the execution status of execution order item is set to Execution Planning.

4. Release the maintenance order after planning, to start execution.

Releasing the maintenance order

5. Create PM confirmations using Fiori app ‘Enter PM Order Confirmation’.

Enter PM Order Confirmation app

6. Fill in the actual work hours taken for the execution of operations and save.

PM Confirmation

Optionally, you can use the Final Confirmation checkbox to indicate that this is the last posting of actuals for an operation.

Final PM Confirmation

7. Open Post Goods Movement Fiori app to post goods issue for the actual components and quantities used.

Goods Issue

8. Open the Create Resource-Related Billing Request - Service Order app or you can use the transaction code DP92 to launch the screen in SAP GUI. Enter the service order ID and the execution order item. Then click Create.

Create Resource- Related Billing Request app

The system displays costs as shown below. The material determination is performed using the DIP profile and actuals are displayed from the data on the maintenance order. The actuals can be seen either in the form of amounts or quantities.

You can set the quantity or the amount to be billed. You can also reject or postpone partially by quantity or amount.

Billing request: Expense view

The prices are determined using SD Pricing and this is shown in Sales Price View.

Billing Request: Sales Price View

9. Select the Billing Request button to generate a debit memo request (DMR).

Create Billing Request

Debit Memo Request in Fiori app

Debit Memo Request

This is another view of the same Debit Memo Request which shows the item categories of the items. L2S here represents a statistical item whereas L2W will be billable. This is the distinct behavior with itemized billing – the main item, which is item 10, will be statistical and the subitems will be considered to calculate net value.

10. The Transfer to Billing is set as Successful and DMR references are updated in the Billing and Transaction History assignment blocks respectively in the execution order item overview page.

Billing assignment block after DMR creation

Transaction history assignment block after DMR creation

11. Open the Create Billing Documents app and search using the Debit Memo Request ID. Select the document from the search result and choose Create Billing Documents.

Create Billing Documents app

Lastly, you can post the Billing Document to accounting, to complete the process.

Post Billing Document

12. Open the Billing assignment block in execution order item again, you can see that the Billing Status is updated as Partially billed.

Billing assignment block after invoice creation

Itemized billing can be performed multiple times for a given execution order item and hence it could lead to multiple debit memo requests depending on number of billing runs. This is typically required when we have a long running service (say, 6 months) and we need to perform billing once every month.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 5 – Late Billing Decision in Maintenance Order
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog: 

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 5:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the maintenance order, and later it is decided to make it billable and thus charge the customer. This is when the service order is created along with an execution order item to bill the customer for the service.

For example, there is a manufacturing company who owns and operates multiple high-tech machines at a customer site under a long-term service contract. The routine maintenance of these machines is taken care by the manufacturing company at no cost to the customer. At a certain instance, the technician from the manufacturing company is performing a routine maintenance activity and created a maintenance order for this purpose. However, during the inspection of the machine, the technician identifies a malfunction caused by mishandling of the equipment. In this case, the technician takes the decision to bill the customer for the resolution of the malfunction.

In such scenarios, when a presumed maintenance needs to be billed, a service order can be created corresponding to an existing maintenance order. Using this service order, the billing document can be generated once the service is completed.

Creation of Billable Maintenance Order

A service order creation can be triggered from a maintenance order by selecting the ‘Billable’ checkbox in the Change Maintenance Order (IW32) screen.

When the ‘Billable’ checkbox is selected you can enter the sales data and service details which includes fields such as sold-to party, sales organization, distribution channel, service product, and quantity. On save, a corresponding service order with an execution order item is created. The lifecycle status of this item is ‘In Process’ and the execution status is ‘Execution Planning’.

The document flow in the service order can be viewed in Transaction History assignment block.

The details of the maintenance order connected to the execution order item can be viewed in the Maintenance Order assignment block in the item details page.

Further, the regular flow and options of the service order processing follows.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

                                                      Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

 

 

Release of Maintenance Order

Once the service order is created from the maintenance order, the maintenance order is in planning phase. After the planning is done, the maintenance order is set to ‘REL’ (Released) by the Maintenance Planner. This automatically triggers the release of the execution order item. With this status transition, the execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘In Execution’.

Technical Completion of Maintenance Order

After the execution of service is completed by the Maintenance Technician, the maintenance order status is set to ‘TECO’ (Technically Completed).  With this status transition, the lifecycle status of execution order item is set to ‘Completed’. The execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘Execution Done’.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the creation of service order from a maintenance order after making it billable for customer. As we proceed with the execution of the job, the lifecycle and execution status of execution order item are in continuous alignment with the status of maintenance order at every step.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: Using Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
entry.content:
You can use Business Event Logging (BEL) to log SAP-delivered events, SAP-extended events, or your events (custom events). 

You can find more information about Business Event Logging here:             

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment  
  • Business Event Logging SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud (Latest) 
  • Business Event Logging SAP S/4 Hana Private Cloud (Latest)                                                        

 There are two ways to define and log custom events in Business Event Logging: 

  • Extend SAP Event 
  • Add Customer Event to SAP Object or Define Customer Event for Customer Object 

Extend SAP Event 

In some scenarios, the information provided by an SAP-delivered event may not suffice to meet business requirements. In such cases, there is a need to augment the event payload with more information. You can use the concept of derived events in the RAP framework to provide more information in the SAP-delivered event. The Business Event Logging framework supports the logging of such extended events. You can refer to the links below to understand how to implement extension via derived events: 

  •   Derived Events 
  •   Prerequisite for Derived Events 

The BEL framework provides an out-of-the-box feature to log all extended events, thereby cutting the need for any manual intervention. You must ensure the framework is active for the given SAP Object Type. 

This functionality is available on: 

         ABAP on BTP (Steampunk) (Latest) 

        SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition (Latest) 

        SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2023 Onwards) 

        SAP S/4 HANA OP 2023 onwards  

Add Customer Event to SAP Object or Define Customer Event for Customer Object 

Business Event Logging has out-of-the-box support for the scenario wherein a customer extends an SAP-delivered Business Object with a Custom event or in pure custom development. RAP has information about how an event can be modeled and raised. You can find the implementation details here. 

Once you have defined an event in RAP Behavior implementation class, you must follow certain prerequisite tasks to ensure that the events are logged in the Business Event Logging framework. 

  1. It is mandatory to add the annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in the abstract entity with the correct SAP Object Node type in case the event is defined at root node in BDEF. If the event is defined at the correct node then maintenance of this annotation is not needed in abstract entity but we need to maintain it in Node CDS entity. The annotation must be in camel case.               

 

     2   In case the event is defined at the root node in BDEF (and not at the correct sub-node), we must add the SAP  Object Node Type Keys (Event.sapObjectNodeTypeKey) via annotations in the Abstract Entity. The order of the keys must be preserved here.     

               

 

   This functionality is available on: 

         ABAP on BTP (Steampunk) (Latest) 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition (Latest) 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2022 FPS2 onwards for Pure Custom Scenario)   

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2023 onwards for SAP Delivered Event Extension)  

         SAP S/4 HANA OP 2022 FPS2 onwards (Pure Custom Scenario)  

          SAP S/4 HANA OP 2023 onwards (For SAP Delivered Event Extension)  

 

Customer Events in the Event Log 

 

 

All SAP-delivered business events use the producer event namespace “sap.s4.beh”.  The Customer events have a different customer provider namespace, according to their definition in the RAP event binding. You can also filter the events by producer event namespace.  

Refer to the below mentioned blog to get an overview of Business Event Log (BEL) framework. 

 https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/one-tool-to-track-them-all/ba-p/13957191

entry.title: Are you ready for asset management capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides comprehensive knowledge on implementation and customizing of technical objects, preventive maintenance and maintenance operations. It is designed to provide consultants with practical skills and solid detail knowledge to execute all required customizing settings in the areas of SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. In addition, consultants will learn about specialized processes and functions.

Learning objectives
Acquire detailed knowledge on how to effectively manage, structure and customize technical objects.

  • Learn and apply preventive maintenance processes, including the management of task lists and the creation and scheduling of various types of maintenance plans.
  • Gain proficiency in customizing settings with a key focus on maintenance notifications, maintenance orders, order costing and order settlement.

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Asset Management

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Transform Your ERP and BW Journey with Our Expert-Led Series
entry.content:
Are you ready to take your ERP and BW transformation to the next level? We’re excited to introduce a series of pre-recorded webcasts designed guide you through the complexities and opportunities of evolving your SAP landscape using SAP Enterprise Support.

Presented in partnership with the UK & Ireland SAP User Group (UKISUG), this e-learning is tailored to SAP customers who are embarking on or progressing through their transformation journeys. Whether you’re just starting or well underway, these sessions will equip you with the knowledge and strategies you need to succeed.

What You'll Gain

By watching these recordings, you’ll:

  • Explore SAP’s essential transformation tools, learning content and services to support your organization’s unique needs.
  • Gain expert insights into critical topics to modernize your systems effectively.
  • Prepare for the future by learning how to avoid common challenges and optimize your investments.

Recording Topics

Here’s a look at the sessions available in this series:

  1. SAP Enterprise Support: Learn what's offered, what's new, and why you should engage.
  2. Maximize your investment with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and SAP Enterprise Support value maps.
  3. The Cost of Inaction: How software obsolescence increases your SAP system's technical debt.
  4. Unlocking the Future: Transform your ECC System to SAP S/4HANA using SAP Enterprise Support.
  5. Safeguard your SAP BW modernization with SAP Enterprise Support.
  6. Maximizing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Benefits with SAP Enterprise Support: Navigating the Transition and Gaining Valuable Assistance.

Start with the First Session

Begin your learning journey with “SAP Enterprise Support: Learn what’s offered, what’s new, and why you should engage.” This session provides an in-depth look at SAP Enterprise Support, our offerings, and how we help organizations like yours achieve transformation success:

Access Recordings

But that’s not all - our commitment to your success goes beyond these recordings. For ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.

Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can:

  • Access curated content and self-service platforms.
  • Engage with fellow customers and SAP experts.
  • Discover continuous quality checks for SAP S/4HANA Cloud in the respective section of the value maps.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access value maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Once in the value maps, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further assistance.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your specific needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Got a question about value maps or our content?

  • Use the “ask questions” feature in the value maps.
  • Or, contact us at: sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support value map team for SAP S/4HANA

entry.content:

Please find below our Q1/2025 ESAC workstreams update and an overview of all our workstreams running in Q1 here. Covering all major cloud solutions, at no additional fee.

We are also actively looking for more customers for the following open workstreams:

To express interest, ask a question or join the program: contact us via esac@sap.com.
